gunslinger.name: Gunslinger
description: ## Gunslinger
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/9;Advanced Class Guide/56›

For a renegade few, battle sounds different than it does for the typical fighter. The clash of steel and the sizzle of spell energy are drowned out by the thunderous rhythm of gunfire - the pounding beat of the gunslinger.

Gunslingers are a bold and mysterious lot. While many treat the secrets of black powder with the same care and reverence that a wizard typically reserves for his spellbook, most gunslingers know that firearms are a secret that cannot remain concealed forever. While current firearms are simple, often imprecise, and even dangerous devices, they are a technology on the move, and one that will become even more powerful when it is fully fused with magic.

**Role:** Gunslingers are thunderous artillery, often found where the fighting is fiercest. Brave, clever, and frequently foolhardy, many gunslingers push to position themselves at close range, barrels blazing, to take down their foes and demoralize their enemies. Other gunslingers are masters of distant death, picking off enemies from afar with their strange and wondrous weapons.
::class{any hd=10 wealth=5 skp=4 acro bluff climb craft ha heal intm ke kl per prof ride soh sur swim}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +1 | +2 | +2 | +0 | Deeds, grit, gunsmith |
| 4th | +4 | +4 | +4 | +1 | Bonus feat |
| 5th | +5 | +4 | +4 | +1 | Gun training 1 |
::prof{simple martial wExtra="all ‹main/equipment_>firearms›" light}

::ab[Gunsmith]{jl=strip l=1 icon=power ability="You gain ‹feat/Gunsmithing› as a bonus feat, and one of the following firearms of your choice: ‹eq-weapon/blunderbuss›, ‹eq-weapon/musket›, or ‹eq-weapon/pistol›. Your starting weapon is battered, and only you know how to use it properly. All other creatures treat your gun as if it had the ‹misc/broken› condition. If the weapon already has the broken condition, it does not work at all for anyone else trying to use it." special="This starting weapon can only be sold for scrap (it's worth 4d10 gp when sold)."}

::ab[Grit (Ex)]{jl=strip flavor="Some gunslingers claim they belong to a mystical way of the gun, but it's more likely that the volatile nature of firearms simply prunes the unlucky and careless from their ranks. Whatever the reason, all gunslingers have grit." icon=power head l=1 ability="In game terms, grit is a fluctuating measure of your ability to perform amazing actions in combat. At the start of each day, you gain a number of grit points equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). Your grit goes up or down throughout the day, but usually cannot go higher than your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1), though some feats and magic items may affect this maximum. You spend grit to accomplish deeds, and regain grit in the following ways."}

::ab[Critical Hit with a Firearm]{icon=power-ranged sub passive="Each time you confirm a critical hit with a firearm attack while in the heat of combat, you regain 1 grit point. Confirming a critical hit on a ‹misc/helpless› or unaware creature or on a creature that has fewer Hit Dice than half your character level does not restore grit."}

::ab[Killing Blow with a Firearm]{sub icon=power-ranged passive="When you reduce a creature to 0 or fewer hit points with a firearm attack while in the heat of combat, you regain 1 grit point. Destroying an unattended object, reducing a helpless or unaware creature to 0 or fewer hit points, or reducing a creature that has fewer Hit Dice than half your character level to 0 or fewer hit points does not restore any grit."}
:::aside
**Optional Rule: Daring Act**
The following method for regaining grit points requires a measure of GM judgment, so it is an optional rule.

**Daring Act:** Each time a gunslinger performs a daring act, she can regain grit. As a general guideline, a daring act should be risky and dramatic. It should take a good deal of guts, and its outcome should have a low probability of success. If it is successful, the gunslinger regains 1 grit point.

Before attempting a daring act, the player should ask the GM whether the act qualifies. The GM is the final arbiter of what's considered a daring act, and can grant a regained grit point for a daring act even if the player does not ask beforehand whether the act qualifies.

::ab[Deeds]{jl=strip icon=power ability="You spend grit points to accomplish deeds. Most deeds grant you some momentary bonus or effect, but there are some that provide longer-lasting effects. Some deeds stay in effect as long as you have at least 1 grit point. Here is the list of base ‹ability/gunslinger deeds›." special="You can only perform deeds of your level or lower. Unless otherwise noted, a deed can be performed multiple successive times, as long as the appropriate amount of grit is spent to perform the deed."}
:::aside
Gunslingers may optionally select a ‹ability/gunslinger_>renowned deed<s› at 1st, 7th and 15th level. Each of these deeds replace two other deeds.

::ab[Nimble (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def passive="You gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC while wearing light or no armor. Anything that causes you to lose your Dexterity bonus to AC also causes you to lose this dodge bonus." imp6="The bonus becomes +2." imp10="The bonus becomes +3." imp14="The bonus becomes +4." imp18="The bonus becomes +5."}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{jl=strip l=4 icon=power ability="You gain a bonus feat in addition to those gained by normal advancement. These bonus feats must be ‹main/combat<_feat› or ‹main/feats_>grit› feats." imp8="You gain another bonus combat or grit feat." imp12="You gain another bonus combat or grit feat." imp16="You gain another bonus combat or grit feat." imp20="You gain another bonus combat or grit feat."}
:::aside
Optionally, a gunslinger can pick a ‹ability/dare<s› instead of a bonus feat. These special abilities only activate when a gunslinger goes down to 0 grit.

::ab[Gun Training (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=boost-ranged passive="You select one specific type of firearm (such as an ‹eq-weapon/axe musket›, blunderbuss, musket, or pistol). You gain a bonus equal to your Dexterity modifier on damage rolls when firing that type of firearm. Furthermore, when you misfire with that type of firearm, the misfire value of that firearm increases by 2 instead of 4." imp9="You select another type of firearm, gaining these same bonuses for it." imp13="You select a third type of firearm, gaining these same bonuses for it." imp17="You select a fourth type of firearm, gaining these same bonuses for it."}

::ab[True Grit (Ex)]{jl=strip l=20 icon=boost ability="You pick two deeds that you have access to and that you must spend grit to perform. You can perform these deeds for 1 grit point fewer (minimum 0) than usual. If the number of grit points to perform a deed is reduced to 0, you can perform this deed as long as you have at least 1 grit point. If a deed could already be performed as long as you had at least 1 grit point, you can now perform that deed even when you have no grit points."}
::h3[Grit and Panache]{jl}

The gunslinger's grit and the ‹class/swashbuckler«'s› panache represent two paths to gain access to the same heroic pool. Characters with both grit and panache class features combine the two resources together into a larger pool of both grit points and panache points.

For purposes of feat prerequisites, a character with the panache class feature satisfies the prerequisites as if she were a grit user, and vice versa. Swashbuckler levels stack with gunslinger levels for the purpose of satisfying ‹feat/Signature Deed«'s› level requirement. For feats, magic items, and other effects, a panache user can spend and gain panache points in place of grit points, and vice versa.
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Personal Armory (Ex)]{oldDogNewTricks capstone="At 20th level, there is not a gun in the world that the gunslinger can't fire. The gunslinger applies her gun training to all firearms, and her damage bonus from gun training increases to 1-1/2 times her Dexterity bonus."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Dwarf:** Reduce the misfire chance for one type of firearm by 1/4. You cannot reduce the misfire chance of a firearm below 1.[^A]
**Elf:** Add +1/3 on critical hit confirmation rolls made with firearms (maximum bonus of +5). This bonus does not stack with ‹feat/Critical Focus›.[^B]
**Fetchling:** Add 1/4 point to the gunslinger's grit.[^C]
**Gnome:** The gunslinger reduces the amount of time needed to restore a broken firearm using the Gunsmithing feat by 5 minutes (maximum reduction of 50 minutes).[^D]
**Goblin:** Add +1/3 on critical hit confirmation rolls made with firearms (maximum bonus of +5). This bonus does not stack with Critical Focus.[^E] [^F]
**Grippli:** Add a +1/4 bonus on attack rolls when making a utility shot or a dead shot.[^G]
**Half-elf:** Add +1/4 to the number of grit points in the gunslinger's grit pool.[^H]
**Halfling:** Add +1/4 to the dodge bonus to AC granted by the nimble class feature (maximum +2) or +1/4 to the AC bonus gained when using the gunslinger's dodge deed.[^I]
**Half-orc:** Add a +1/3 bonus on attack rolls when using the pistol whip deed.[^J]
**Hobgoblin:** Add +1/4 to the gunslinger's grit points.[^K]
**Human:** Add +1/4 point to the gunslinger's grit points.[^L]
**Ifrit:** Add +1/2 to the bonus on initiative checks the gunslinger makes while using her gunslinger initiative deed.[^M]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^N]
**Kobold:** Add +1/4 to the dodge bonus to AC granted by the nimble class feature (maximum +4).[^O]
**Ratfolk:** Add a +1/2 bonus on initiative checks when the gunslinger has at least 1 grit point.[^P]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Black Powder Vaulter]{c=gunslinger r="Deadeye; Gunslinger's Dodge; Gunslinger Initiative; Startling Shot; Expert Loading" e="Black powder vaulters like to get into the thick of the battle, maneuvering among the fray to find the most advantageous positions from which to kill their enemies."}

::archetype[Blatherskite]{c=gunslinger r="Class Skills; Gunslinger's Dodge; Gunslinger Initiative; Pistol-Whip; Startling Shot" e="The blatherskate has an extraordinary talent for retreating from harm's way and fooling his enemies into thinking he is weaker than he is."}

::archetype[Bolt Ace]{c=gunslinger r="Weapon Proficiencies; Gunsmith; Grit; Deadeye; Quick Clear; Utility Shot; Startling Shot; Expert Loading; Lightning Reload; Menacing Shot; Gun Training" e="While gunslingers are full of sound and fury, there is a class of gunslingers that never soil their hands with powder or feel the sting of gun smoke."}

::archetype[Buccaneer]{c=gunslinger r="Quick Clear Deed; Pistol Whip Deed; Dead Shot Deed; Lightning Reload Deed; Grit; Nimble; Gun Training 1, 2, and 4" e="(Human Only) Freebooters who cling to the convoluted codes that rule independent ships, the buccaneer is a gunslinger of the high seas."}

::archetype[Bushwhacker]{c=gunslinger r="Grit; Utility Shot Deed; Targeting Deed; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, 20th-level Bonus Feat" e="(Kobold Only) The bushwhacker specializes in the art of the ambush. For her, gunplay works best when it comes from a concealed position and is directed against a target that falls with the very first volley and is dead before the smoke clears."}

::archetype[Commando]{c=gunslinger r="Class Skills; Utility Shot; Expert Loading; Lightning Reload; Slinger's Luck; 4th-level Bonus Feat; Gun Training" e="Commandos are those rough and ready gunslingers who excel at hit-and-run tactics and leveraging their expertise in the wild against their enemies."}

::archetype[Experimental Gunsmith]{c=gunslinger r="Gunsmith; Gunsmithing; Gun Training" e="(Gnome Only) The experimental gunsmith is obsessed with creating the ultimate firearm, and is willing to take risks to work the kinks out of her design."}

::archetype[Firebrand]{c=gunslinger r="Gunsmith; Grit; Deadeye; Dead Shot; Bonus Feats; Gun Training" e="Where other gunslingers rely on precision shooting, the firebrand instead masters explosive ordnance."}

::archetype[Graveslinger]{c=gunslinger r="Deadeye; Startling Shot; Bleeding Wound; Menacing Shot; 4th-level Bonus Feat" e="Graveslingers craft signature bullets to hunt down wandering undead. With this infused ammunition, they can take on the most resilient spectral foes with ease."}

::archetype[Gulch Gunner]{c=gunslinger r="Class Skills; Grit; Deadeye Deed; Pistol Whip Deed; Menacing Shot Deed; 9th, 13th, 17th-level Gun Training" e="(Ratfolk Only) These gulch gunners often specialize in proficiency with a single pistol (easily handled in tight spaces) and wander from warren to warren selling their tunnel-shooting skills to the highest bidder."}

::archetype[Gun Scavenger]{c=gunslinger r="Gunsmith; Quick Clear Deed; Targeting Deed; Nimble" e="A gun scavenger collects scraps, spare parts, and other components that might - if modified properly - give her firearm an extra edge."}

::archetype[Gun Tank]{c=gunslinger r="Armor Proficiency; Gunslinger's Dodge Deed; Evasive Deed; Nimble; Bonus Feats (4th level and every 4 levels after)" e="Gunslingers usually don't use heavy armor, but there are a few who use and modify their armor to protect themselves from both traditional weapons and gunfire."}
::archetype[Gunner Squire]{c=gunslinger r="Gunslinger's Dodge; Gunslinger Initiative; Nimble +1" e="The gunner squire is prepared to reload or repair her lord's firearms at a moment's notice."}

::archetype[Maverick]{c=gunslinger r="Dodge; Pistol Whip; Gunslinger Initiative" e="Quick with her gun and no stranger to barroom brawls, a maverick can hold her own when bullets and punches start flying."}

::archetype[Musket Master]{c=gunslinger r="Weapon Proficiency; Gunsmith; Gunslinger's Dodge Deed; Utility Shot Deed; Gun Training 1-4" e="Some gunslingers want a little more firepower and range. These are the musket masters; the best of these gunslingers can reload a musket with dazzling speed, potentially putting even their pistol-wielding brethren to shame."}

::archetype[Mysterious Stranger]{c=gunslinger r="Grit; Quick Clear Deed; Nimble; Gun Training 1" e="A few rare gunslingers seem to accomplish their chosen tasks purely through willpower and an unwillingness to ever give up."}

::archetype[Pistolero]{c=gunslinger r="Weapon Proficiency; Gunsmith; Deadeye Deed; Startling Shot Deed; Menacing Shot Deed; Gun Training 1-4" e="While most gunslingers have favorite firearms, there are those rare ones who choose to specialize in one-handed firearms exclusively."}

::archetype[Planar Rifter]{c=gunslinger r="Grit; Deadeye; Dead Shot; Menacing Shot; Death's Shot; Nimble; Gun Training" e="Planar rifters harvest pieces of the Outer Planes, absorbing some of the energy into themselves and condensing other portions of it into bullets they can fire from their guns"}

::archetype[Scatter Gunner]{c=gunslinger r="Gunsmith; Deadeye; Utility Shot; Dead Shot; Targeting" e="Punishing firearms such as the blunderbuss and dragon pistol can lay waste to invaders in tight corridors, and the dwarves of Dongun Hold have pioneered masterful methods for making the most of these indiscriminate weapons"}

::archetype[Siege Gunner]{c=gunslinger r="Grit; Nimble; Deadeye; Gunslinger Initiative; Bonus Feats" e="The Grand Duchy of Alkenstar has spawned all manner of firearm specialists, wielding small and large firearms alike. Siege gunners specialize in the very largest: cannons, bombards, and firedrakes."}
::archetype[Techslinger]{c=gunslinger r="Deadeye; Quick Clear; Expert Loading; Gun Training" e="Techslingers spurn unreliable gunpowder weapons in favor of high-tech armaments."}
::archetype[Thronewarden]{c=gunslinger r="Class Skills; Nimble; 4th-level Bonus Feat; Startling Shot; Bleeding Wound" e="As vigilant sentinels, thronewardens identify and head off trouble."}

::archetype[Wyrm Sniper]{c=gunslinger r="Class Skills; Starling Shot Deed; Lightning Reload Deed; Menacing Shot Deed" e="These long-shot artists are masters at taking down dragons with sharp shooting and light siege weaponry."}

barbarian.name: Barbarian
description: ## Barbarian
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/31›

For some, there is only rage. In the ways of their people, in the fury of their passion, in the howl of battle, conflict is all these brutal souls know. Savages, hired muscle, masters of vicious martial techniques, they are not soldiers or professional warriors - they are the battle possessed, creatures of slaughter and spirits of war. Known as barbarians, these warmongers know little of training, preparation, or the rules of warfare; for them, only the moment exists, with the foes that stand before them and the knowledge that the next moment might hold their death. They possess a sixth sense in regard to danger and the endurance to weather all that might entail. These brutal warriors might rise from all walks of life, both civilized and savage, though whole societies embracing such philosophies roam the wild places of the world. Within barbarians storms the primal spirit of battle, and woe to those who face their rage.

**Role:** Barbarians excel in combat, possessing the martial prowess and fortitude to take on foes seemingly far superior to themselves. With rage granting them boldness and daring beyond that of most other warriors, barbarians charge furiously into battle and ruin all who would stand in their way.
::class{al="Any nonlawful" hd=12 wealth=3 ng n ne cg cn ce skp=4 acro climb craft ha intm kna per ride sur swim}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +1 | +2 | +0 | +0 | Fast movement, rage |
| 2nd | +2 | +3 | +0 | +0 | Rage power, uncanny dodge |
| 4th | +4 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Rage power |
| 5th | +5 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Improved uncanny dodge |
| 6th | +6/+1 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Rage power, Trap sense +2 |
| 7th | +7/+2 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Damage reduction 1/- |
| 10th | +10/+5 | +7 | +3 | +3 | Damage reduction 2/-, Rage power |
| 12th | +12/+7/+2 | +8 | +4 | +4 | Rage power, Trap sense +4 |
| 14th | +14/+9/+4 | +9 | +4 | +4 | Indomitable will, Rage power |
| 16th | +16/+11/+6/+1 | +10 | +5 | +5 | Damage reduction 4/-, Rage power |
| 20th | +20/+15/+10/+5 | +12 | +6 | +6 | Mighty rage, Rage power |
**Weapon and Armor Proficiency:** A barbarian is proficient with all simple and martial weapons, light armor, medium armor, and shields (except tower shields).
::prof{simple martial light medium shields}

::ab[Fast Movement (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost passive="Your land speed is faster than the norm for your race by +10 feet." special="This benefit applies only when you are wearing no armor, light armor, or medium armor, and not carrying a heavy load. Apply this bonus before modifying your speed because of any load carried or armor worn. This bonus stacks with any other bonuses to your land speed."}

::ab[Rage (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=power-boost flavor="A barbarian can call upon inner reserves of strength and ferocity, granting her additional combat prowess." l=1 usage="2 rounds/day + double your barbarian level + your Constitution modifier" free="You can enter (or end) a rage. You gain a +4 morale bonus to your Strength and Constitution, as well as a +2 morale bonus on Will saves. The increase to Constitution grants you 2 hit points per Hit Dice, but these disappear when the rage ends and are not lost first like ‹rule/temporary hit points›." order=ufp passive="While in rage, you take a -2 penalty to Armor Class, and you cannot use any Charisma-, Dexterity-, or Intelligence-based skills (except Acrobatics, Fly, Intimidate, and Ride) or any ability that requires patience or concentration. You are ‹misc/fatigued› after *rage* for a number of rounds equal to 2 times the number of rounds spent in the rage. You cannot enter a new *rage* while fatigued or ‹misc/exhausted› but can otherwise enter *rage* multiple times during a single encounter or combat. If you fall unconscious, your rage immediately ends, placing you in peril of death." special="Temporary increases to Constitution, such as those gained from rage and spells like ‹spell/bear's endurance›, do not increase the total number of rounds that a barbarian can rage per day. The total number of rounds of rage per day is renewed after resting for 8 hours, although these hours do not need to be consecutive."}

::ab[Rage Powers (Ex)]{jl=strip flavor="As a barbarian gains levels, she learns to use her rage in new ways." l=2 icon=power-boost-def ability="You gain a ‹ability/rage power<s›. You gain another rage power at every even-numbered level of barbarian after this (4th, 6th, 8th, and so on)." special="You gain the benefits of rage powers only while raging, and some of these powers require you to take an action first. Unless otherwise noted, you cannot select an individual power more than once."}

::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def passive="You cannot be caught ‹rule/flat-footed›, nor do you lose your Dex bonus to AC if the attacker is invisible." special="You still lose your Dexterity bonus to AC if immobilized, or if an opponent successfully uses the feint action against you. If you have *uncanny dodge* from a different class, you automatically gain *improved uncanny dodge* instead."}

::ab[Trap Sense (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=def passive="You gain a +1 bonus on Reflex saves made to avoid traps and a +1 dodge bonus to AC against attacks made by traps." special="*Trap sense* bonuses gained from multiple classes stack." imp6="These bonuses become +2." imp9="These bonuses become +3." imp12="These bonuses become +4." imp15="These bonuses become +5." imp18="These bonuses become +6."}

::ab[Improved Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=def passive="You can no longer be ‹rule/flanked›. This defense denies a rogue the ability to ‹ability/sneak attack› you by flanking you, unless the attacker has at least four more rogue levels than you have barbarian levels." special="If you already have *uncanny dodge* from another class, the levels from the classes that grant *uncanny dodge* stack to determine the minimum rogue level required to flank you."}

::ab[Damage Reduction (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=def l=7 passive="You gain ‹rule/damage reduction› of DR 1/-." imp10="You now have DR 2/-." imp13="You now have DR 3/-." imp16="You now have DR 4/-." imp19="You now have DR 5/-."}

::ab[Greater Rage (Ex)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=11 icon=boost-def free="When you enter a *rage,* the morale bonus to your Strength and Constitution increase to +6 and the morale bonus on your Will saves increases to +3."}

::ab[Indomitable Will (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def icon=def passive="You gain a +4 bonus on Will saves to resist enchantment spells. This bonus stacks with all other modifiers, including the morale bonus on Will saves you also receive during your *rage.*"}
::ab[Tireless Rage (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost passive="You no longer become fatigued at the end of your *rage.*"}
::ab[Mighty Rage (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost-def free="When you enter a *rage,* the morale bonus to your Strength and Constitution increase to +8 and the morale bonus on your Will saves increases to +4."}
::h3[Ex-Barbarians]{jl}
A barbarian who becomes lawful loses the ability to rage and cannot gain more levels as a barbarian. She retains all other benefits of the class.
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Unstoppable (Ex)]{capstone="At 20th level, nothing can kill the barbarian, though not for lack of trying. The barbarian gains DR 3/- or increases the value of any existing damage reduction by 3. In addition, she gains energy resistance to acid, cold, electricity, and fire equal to her DR/- value."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Boggard:** Add 1 on the barbarian's Acrobatics checks to jump.[^A]
**Dwarf:** Add +1 to the barbarian's total number of rage rounds per day.[^B] [^C]

**Elf:** Add +1 to the barbarian's base speed. In combat this option has no effect unless the barbarian has selected it five times (or another increment of five). This bonus stacks with the barbarian's fast movement feature and applies under the same conditions as that feature.[^D] [^E]
**Fetchling:** Add +1 to either cold or electricity resistance while raging (maximum resistance 15 for either type).[^F]
**Gathlain:** Add 1/4 to the barbarian's damage reduction. If the barbarian would not otherwise have damage reduction, this damage reduction can be bypassed by cold iron.[^G]
**Gnome:** Add a +1/2 bonus to the barbarian's trap sense.[^H]
**Goblin:** Add +1/2 on critical hit confirmation rolls for attacks made with unarmed strikes or natural weapons (maximum bonus of +4). This bonus does not stack with ‹feat/Critical Focus›.[^I]
**Grippli:** Add 1/5 to the armor bonus granted by hide armor or bone armor the barbarian wears (maximum +3).[^J]
**Half-elf:** Add +1/4 to the bonus on Reflex saves and dodge bonus to AC against attacks made by traps granted by trap sense.[^K]
**Halfling:** Add a +1/2 bonus to trap sense or +1/3 to the bonus from the surprise accuracy rage power.[^L]
**Halfling:** Add 1 foot to the range increment of thrown weapons the barbarian wields. This option has no effect unless the barbarian has selected it 5 times (or another increment of 5).[^M]
**Half-orc:** Add +1 to the barbarian's total number of rage rounds per day.[^N] [^O]
**Human:** Add a +1/2 bonus to trap sense or +1/3 to the bonus from the superstitious rage power.[^P] [^Q]
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^R]
**Kobold:** While you are raging, your racial natural attacks deal +1/4 point of damage.[^S]
**Orc:** Add +1 to the barbarian's total number of rage rounds per day.[^T]
**Ratfolk:** When raging, add +1/4 to the barbarian's swarming trait's flanking bonus on attack rolls.[^U]
**Strix:** Add +1 to the barbarian's total number of rage rounds per day.[^V]
**Tengu:** Add +1/3 to the bonus from the superstitious rage power.[^W]
**Wayang:** Add a +1/4 bonus to weapon damage rolls that the barbarian makes against opponents that are within ‹rule/dim light› or darkness.[^X]
::h3[Archetypes]{jl}

::archetype[Armored Hulk]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Some barbarians disdain the hides and leather used as armor by most of their kin. Instead they master the heaviest of armors, even those created by more civilized people, to gain greater protection and stability in battle."}

::archetype[Beastkin Berserker]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; 4th, 8th, 12th-level Rage Power" e="While some barbarians take on bestial aspects in their rages, the beastkin berserker descends so deeply into primal fury that she actually transforms into an animal."}
::archetype[Breaker]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Trap Sense" e="Breakers find the need to destroy their surroundings."}
::archetype[Brutal Pugilist]{c=barbarian r="Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Some barbarians focus on using their bare hands to tear their opponents limb from limb."}

::archetype[Brutish Swamper]{c=barbarian r="Skills; Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; 6th-level Rage Power" e="The hardy and insular denizens of the swamp produce warriors of narrow focus and great strength."}

::archetype[Cave Dweller]{c=barbarian r="Skills; Fast Movement; Trap Sense; Damage Reduction" e="Unused to the light of the sun, cave dwellers use their subterranean expertise to protect their clans from the manifold dangers of deep caverns and tunnels."}

::archetype[Deepwater Rager]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Indomitable Will; Tireless Rage" e="Deepwater ragers bellow ferociously as they charge their enemies, tackling them into the raging seas."}

::archetype[Dreadnought]{c=barbarian r="Rage; Fast Movement; Indomitable Will; Tireless Rage" e="Unstoppable and fearless, the dreadnought turns the terror of her enemies into violent physical power."}
::archetype[Drunken Brute]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement" e="Drunken brutes use potent liquor to fuel their rage and grant them additional powers."}

::archetype[Drunken Rager]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 12th-level Rage Power" e="These hotheaded, hard-drinking ruffians and brawlers are as dangerous in the midst of combat as they are in a tavern, and they wouldn't be caught dead without a libation for either occasion."}
::archetype[Elemental Kin]{c=barbarian r="Trap Sense" e="Some barbarian tribes have strong ties to the elemental forces of nature."}

::archetype[Fearsome Defender]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="These savage slaves feel little pain and exist only to rain terror down on the enemies of their pitiless masters."}

::archetype[Feral Gnasher]{c=barbarian r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Fast Movement; 2nd-level Rage Power; Trap Sense 1-5; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="(Goblin Only) Feral gnashers grow up in the wild, either raised by animals or scraping by on their own, and soon learn to fend for themselves."}

::archetype[Flesheater]{c=barbarian r="Rage; 2nd, 8th, and 14th-level Rage Powers; Greater Rage; Mighty Rage" e="A flesheater eats flesh to create a spiritual bond between herself and the consumed creature, allowing her to take on aspects of the creature that served as the meal."}

::archetype[Geminate Invoker]{c=barbarian r="Alignment; Class Skills; Rage; Trap Sense; 4th, 8th, 12th-level Rage Powers" e="Geminate invokers are barbarians who use Rivethun traditions to invite spirits into their bodies."}
::archetype[Giant Stalker]{c=barbarian r="Rage; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense" e="Giant stalkers are followers of the Mammoth Lords who have trained since childhood to spot and track giants."}

::archetype[Hateful Rager]{c=barbarian r="Rage; 2nd, 8th, 14th, 20th-level Rage Powers; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense +3" e="(Half-Orc Only) From a young age, many half-orcs are treated cruelly, bullied, ridiculed, and made outcasts. While some hide their shame, others foster a deep, burning hatred that they channel into a raw fury and unleash against their enemies."}
::archetype[Hurler]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement" e="The hurler becomes skilled at throwing objects at their foes before closing in for the kill."}

::archetype[Invulnerable Rager]{c=barbarian r="Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction; Trap Sense" e="Some barbarians learn to take whatever comes their way, shrugging off mortal wounds with ease."}

::archetype[Jungle Rager]{c=barbarian r="Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction" e="A jungle rager can disappear from sight, strike from hiding, and use the environment to shield herself from harm."}

::archetype[Mad Dog]{c=barbarian r="2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, 18th-level Rage Powers; Rage; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction; Indomitable Will" e="Though named for the wild savages who fight alongside rabid dogs, mad dogs employ all manner of beasts as their battle brethren."}

::archetype[Mooncursed]{c=barbarian r="Rage; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Greater Rage; Mighty Rage" e="Some barbarians exhibit an unusual form of lycanthropy powered by their fury. Such a character transforms willfully but can maintain her animal or hybrid forms only while raging."}

::archetype[Mounted Fury]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Many barbarian tribes are masters of the horse, teaching their members how to ride from a young age."}

::archetype[Numerian Liberator]{c=barbarian r="Class Skills; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Indomitable Will" e="While many native Kellids oppose the Technic League and its espousal of foul alien artifacts, the most stalwart and staunchly dedicated of these technophobic traditionalists refer to themselves as Numerian liberators."}

::archetype[Pack Hunter]{c=barbarian r="Rage Powers; Trap Sense; 6th-level Rage Power" e="Pack hunter barbarians team up to hunt dangerous prey to feed, to protect their camps, and as a rite of passage."}

::archetype[Pack Rager]{c=barbarian r="2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th-level Rage Powers; Damage Reduction" e="Barbarian rages can be a thing of savage beauty, exhibiting a lethal grace. While such uncontrolled displays of carnage often disregard group tactics, there are those barbarians whose rages inspire and spur on their allies during the ferocious dance of death."}
::archetype[Primal Hunter]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Rage" e="Rather than exploding with anger, primal hunters focus their rage to strike distant targets."}

::archetype[Raging Cannibal]{c=barbarian r="2nd-level Rage Power; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction" e="While savagery is not inherently evil, some barbaric cultures thrive on depravity and welcome the act of feeding on their own kind."}
::archetype[Savage Barbarian]{c=barbarian r="Trap Sense; Damage Reduction" e="The savage barbarian learns to avoid blows and toughen up their skin."}

::archetype[Savage Technologist]{c=barbarian r="Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Rage; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Savage technologists exhort Kellids to rise up against the depredations of the Technic League. But rather than rejecting technology, they wield the League's own weapons against it."}
::archetype[Scarred Rager]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="The scarred rager believes each wound tells the tales of her prowess and bravery."}

::archetype[Sea Reaver]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Some sea reavers are no more than hunters of the open sea, while others are raiders striking fear into coastal settlements within reach of the sea reavers' longships."}

::archetype[Sharptooth]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; 6th and 12th-level Rage Powers" e="Coastal hunters and agents of the ocean's rage, sharptooth barbarians emulate the greatest predators of the sea."}

::archetype[Shoanti Burn Rider]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Rage Powers; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="The Shoanti tribes who live in the Cinderlands have turned the deadly emberstorms that plague the region into a rite of passage. A few who survive the experience begin to embrace the adrenaline and surge of joy that comes from dancing along the edge of a whirling firestorm. These thrill-seekers are known as burn riders, and their exploits are celebrated by all Shoanti."}
::archetype[Superstitious]{c=barbarian r="Trap Sense; Damage Reduction" e="The superstitious barbarian is naturally distrusting, developing keen senses to protect themselves from harm."}

::archetype[Titan Mauler]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Indomitable Will" e="In lands overrun by giants, dragons, and other hulking beasts, entire fellowships of barbarians hone tactics and traditions with one purpose - to bring low these massive foes."}
::archetype[Totem Warrior]{c=barbarian r=None e="The totem warrior has a special totem that is the patron of her tribe."}

::archetype[True Primitive]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Trap Sense" e="Isolated and xenophobic tribes that dwell in areas untouched by civilization often see anything from cities and organized settlements as strange, dangerous, and decadent."}

::archetype[Untamed Rager]{c=barbarian r="Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction" e="There are no rules in the wild. Some barbarians enter combat with only victory in mind and do anything in their power to achieve it."}

::archetype[Urban Barbarian]{c=barbarian r="Fast Movement; Rage" e="Every barbarian knows that city life can soften the spirit and the body, but some barbarians take on the trappings and ways of their adoptive homes and bend their savage powers to its challenges."}

::archetype[Wild Rager]{c=barbarian r="Rage; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge" e="Rages are barely controlled, but there are those who wholly give in to their more savage side, letting their rages take them to a confusing and uncontrolled place of terrible savagery."}

::archetype[Wildborn]{c=barbarian r="Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Trap Sense; Rage Powers; Damage Reduction" e="Some barbarians are born or bred outside the reach of civilization. These wildborn fight and survive without ever seeing a forge or worked stone, and only dare a trip into the city when no other option is available."}

unchained_barbarian.name: Unchained Barbarian
description: ## Unchained Barbarian
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Unchained/8›

For some heroes, the rage that lives within them can't be contained. Whether this fury is a product of heritage or of nature, these adventurers have learned to harness it, turning its power against their foes. The primal warriors known as barbarians transform their rage into a potent weapon on a battlefield. Heedless of their wounds and possessed of unstoppable bloodlust, these savage brutes are the embodiment of war.

**Unchained:** The unchained barbarian is similar in many regards to its counterpart in the Core Rulebook - she still goes into a rage and gains rage powers, but many of the details have been changed. Rage now grants ‹rule/temporary hit points› and static bonuses on attack and damage rolls, rather than bonuses to ability scores that force players to recalculate a number of statistics. Additionally, many rage powers now function whenever the barbarian is raging, as opposed to just once per rage. Finally, the overall utility of many of the rage powers has been adjusted to make them more useful and interesting.
::class{al="Any nonlawful" ng n ne cg cn ce hd=12 wealth=3 skp=4 acro climb craft ha intm kna per ride sur swim}
### Class Features
| Level | Base Attack Bonus | Fort Save | Ref Save | Will Save | Special |
| 1st | +1 | +2 | +0 | +0 | Fast movement, rage |
| 2nd | +2 | +3 | +0 | +0 | Rage power, uncanny dodge |
| 3rd | +3 | +3 | +1 | +1 | Danger sense +1 |
| 4th | +4 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Rage power |
| 5th | +5 | +4 | +1 | +1 | Improved uncanny dodge |
| 6th | +6/+1 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Danger sense +2, rage power |
| 7th | +7/+2 | +5 | +2 | +2 | Damage reduction 1/- |
| 10th | +10/+5 | +7 | +3 | +3 | Damage reduction 2/-, rage power |
| 12th | +12/+7/+2 | +8 | +4 | +4 | Danger sense +4, rage power |
| 14th | +14/+9/+4 | +9 | +4 | +4 | Indomitable will, rage power |
| 16th | +16/+11/+6/+1 | +10 | +5 | +5 | Damage reduction 4/-, rage power |
| 18th | +18/+13/+8/+3 | +11 | +6 | +6 | Danger sense +6, rage power |
| 20th | +20/+15/+10/+5 | +12 | +6 | +6 | Mighty rage, rage power |
::prof{simple martial light medium shields}

::ab[Fast Movement (Ex)]{jl=strip l=1 icon=boost passive="Your land speed is faster than the norm for your race by +10 feet." special="This benefit applies only when you are wearing no armor, light armor, or medium armor, and not carrying a heavy load. Apply this bonus before modifying your speed because of any load carried or armor worn. This bonus stacks with any other bonuses to your land speed."}

::ab[Rage (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=power-boost-def flavor="A barbarian can call upon inner reserves of strength and ferocity, granting her additional combat prowess." l=1 usage="2 rounds/day + double your barbarian level + your Constitution modifier" free="You can enter (or end) a rage. You gain a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls, melee damage rolls, thrown weapon damage rolls, and Will saving throws. You gain 2 ‹rule/temporary hit points› per Hit Die. These temporary hit points are lost first when a character takes damage, disappear when the rage ends, and are not replenished if you enter a rage again within 1 minute of your previous rage." order=ufp passive="While in rage, you take a -2 penalty to Armor Class, and you cannot use any Charisma-, Dexterity-, or Intelligence-based skills (except Acrobatics, Fly, Intimidate, and Ride) or any ability that requires patience or concentration. You are ‹misc/fatigued› for 1 minute after the *rage* ends. You cannot enter a new *rage* while fatigued or ‹misc/exhausted› but can otherwise enter *rage* multiple times during a single encounter or combat. If you fall unconscious, your rage immediately ends." special="Temporary increases to Constitution, such as those gained from rage and spells like ‹spell/bear's endurance›, do not increase the total number of rounds that a barbarian can rage per day. The total number of rounds of rage per day is renewed after resting for 8 hours, although these hours do not need to be consecutive."}

::ab[Rage Powers (Ex)]{jl=strip flavor="As a barbarian gains levels, she learns to use her rage in new ways." l=2 icon=power-boost ability="You gain a ‹ability/rage power<s›. You gain another rage power at every even-numbered level of barbarian after this (4th, 6th, 8th, and so on). You gain the benefits of rage powers only while raging, and some of these powers require you to take an action first. Unless otherwise noted, you cannot select an individual power more than once." special="Some rage powers are ***stances.*** Activating a ‹ability/stance rage power<s› is a ***move action.*** You can't have more than one stance rage power active at a time. If you activate a stance rage power while another one is active, the current stance immediately ends. The stance can be intentionally ended at the beginning of your turn as a ***free action;*** otherwise, it lasts until the rage ends." hSpecial}

::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=2 icon=def passive="You cannot be caught ‹rule/flat-footed›, nor do you lose your Dex bonus to AC if the attacker is invisible." special="You still lose your Dexterity bonus to AC if immobilized, or if an opponent successfully uses the feint action against you. If you have *uncanny dodge* from a different class, you automatically gain *improved uncanny dodge* instead."}

::ab[Danger Sense (Ex)]{jl=strip l=3 icon=def passive="You gain a +1 bonus on Reflex saves made to avoid traps, a +1 dodge bonus to AC against attacks made by traps, and a +1 bonus on Perception checks to avoid being surprised by a foe." special="This ability counts as *trap sense* for any feat or class prerequisite, and can be replaced by any archetype class feature that replaces *trap sense.* The bonuses gained from this ability stack with those gained from *trap sense* (if you have *trap sense* from another class)." imp6="These bonuses become +2." imp9="These bonuses become +3." imp12="These bonuses become +4." imp15="These bonuses become +5." imp18="These bonuses become +6."}

::ab[Improved Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{jl=strip l=5 icon=def passive="You can no longer be ‹rule/flanked›. This defense denies enemies the ability to ‹ability/sneak attack› you by flanking you, unless the attacker has at least four more levels in a class that provides *sneak attack* than you have barbarian levels." special="If you already have *uncanny dodge* from another class, the levels from the classes that grant *uncanny dodge* stack to determine the minimum rogue level required to flank you."}

::ab[Damage Reduction (Ex)]{jl=strip icon=def l=7 passive="You gain ‹rule/damage reduction› of DR 1/-." imp10="You now have DR 2/-." imp13="You now have DR 3/-." imp16="You now have DR 4/-." imp19="You now have DR 5/-."}

::ab[Greater Rage (Ex)]{jl="Other Abilities" l=11 icon=boost-def free="The bonus on melee attack rolls, melee damage rolls, thrown weapon damage rolls, and Will saves while raging increases to +3. In addition, the amount of temporary hit points gained when entering a *rage* increases to 3 per Hit Die."}

::ab[Indomitable Will (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def icon=def passive="You gain a +4 bonus on Will saves to resist enchantment spells. This bonus stacks with all other modifiers, including the morale bonus on Will saves you also receive during your *rage.*"}

::ab[Tireless Rage (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost passive="You no longer become fatigued at the end of your *rage.* If you enter a *rage* again within 1 minute of ending a *rage,* you don't gain any temporary hit points from your *rage.*"}

::ab[Mighty Rage (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost-def free="When you enter a *rage,* the bonus on melee attack rolls, melee damage rolls, thrown weapon damage rolls, and Will saves while raging increases to +4. In addition, the amount of temporary hit points gained when entering a rage increases to 4 per Hit Die."}
::h3[Alternate Capstones]{jl}
‹SOURCE Chronicle of Legends/28›
@altCapstoneDesc

::altCapstone[Unstoppable (Ex)]{capstone="At 20th level, nothing can kill the barbarian, though not for lack of trying. The barbarian gains DR 3/- or increases the value of any existing damage reduction by 3. In addition, she gains energy resistance to acid, cold, electricity, and fire equal to her DR/- value."}
::h3[Favored Class Bonuses]{jl}
:::div{className="hanging striped"}
**Kitsune:** Gain 1/6 of a new Magical Tail feat. Any kitsune character can choose this bonus upon gaining a level in her favored class.[^A]
